prompt.instructions:
Identify relationships between these entities based on the content below.
<entities> {entity_list} </entities>
<content> {content} </content>
ALLOWED RELATIONSHIP TYPES (source type -> target type): {relationship_schema}
Return ONLY a YAML block — no preamble, no commentary:
```yaml relationships: - source: "entity-id-1" target: "entity-id-2" type: "reports_to" confidence: 0.9 description: "Barry reports to Stephen" evidence: "Since I report to Stephen it keeps the escalation path simple" ```
Rules: - source and target MUST be entity IDs from the entities list above - type MUST be one of the allowed relationship types, and the source/target entity types MUST match that relationship's signature — a person works_on a project, never an account - Direction matters: "X reports_to Y" means X is the subordinate. "Y is X's manager" also means X reports_to Y. Never emit the reversed edge. - Use the MOST SPECIFIC type that fits: a reporting relationship is reports_to, NOT collaborates_with; an account run by a person is managed_by, NOT works_on - evidence: a short quote from the content that states the relationship. Only include relationships the content actually states or unambiguously implies — no inferences from mere co-occurrence in the same meeting - confidence: 0.0-1.0. Below 0.7 means the content does not clearly state the relationship — prefer omitting it - Do NOT emit relationships for: former employers or anecdotes, hypothetical staffing, skip-level chains (if X reports to Y and Y reports to Z, do not emit X reports_to Z), or joke/banter topics - Emit each relationship exactly once — NEVER also emit its inverse (belongs_to_account and has_projects are the same edge; pick the direction listed in the allowed types and move on) - collaborates_with requires evidence of people actually working together on shared work. Speaking in the same meeting, running the agenda, or asking someone for a status update is NOT collaboration - A reporting or management relationship is NEVER also collaborates_with — emit only the hierarchical edge - If a relationship you observe has no allowed type, OMIT it — never substitute a looser type - If no relationships found, return: relationships: [] - Quote all string values
Verify before responding: every triple's source/target types match the relationship signature, every evidence quote appears in the content, and no reversed reporting edges exist.
